commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

commit.message:
feat: full feature implementation + architecture hardening

Features implemented (all using free/no-key tools):
- Ad Generate: accepts keyword/platform/goal, Anthropic→Gemini→Ollama→HF chain with per-provider timeouts
- Keyword Research: GET /api/v1/seo/keywords/research — Google Autocomplete + DDG Suggest + Google Trends + AI insights
- Competitor Analyze: POST /api/v1/competitors/analyze — Puppeteer crawl + AI analysis
- Pulse Monitoring: GET/POST /api/v1/pulse/* — node-cron (15min), threshold eval, notifications
- Integration Status: GET /api/v1/integrations/status — shows all connected AI/ad keys

Architecture (per spec):
- src/cache/index.js — node-cache layer (30min AI, 2hr keyword TTLs)
- src/orchestrators/adsOrchestrator.js — cache-first ad generation
- src/middleware/timing.js — responseTime injected into every JSON response
- src/utils/timeout.js + retry.js — withTimeout/withRetry helpers

- localStorage save/progress system
- Pure HTML5 + CSS + vanilla JS, no frameworks
- SVG art, CSS animations, DOM rendering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign InMemoryMarketDataService with dense arrays and service caching

Replace HashMap-per-price-point storage with per-instrument dense Vec<f64>
arrays indexed by date arithmetic (O(1) lookups, NaN for missing values).
Add freeze() two-phase construction, from_bulk/from_dense constructors,
and bincode+lz4 service serialization for instant warm starts.

Key results:
- Njorda startup: ~40s → 0.66s (warm), ~4s (cold build + save)
- Volatility endpoint: 177ms → 0.4ms (with_market_data zero-copy borrow)
- Stats endpoint: 12ms → 0.4ms (cached instrument_count)
- Memory: 1012 MB RSS for 57M prices across 105K instruments

Also includes: DataLoader abstraction, permissions module, Outcome<T>
for partial results with warnings, njorda cache layer (rkyv+lz4),
expanded API routes, updated docs and CLAUDE.md files.

fix(app): fix settleAndRedeem always reporting failure (#1243)

sendCalls resolves before the onTxHash callback fires, so
successRef.current was never set to true for batched calls.
Return { success: true } directly since sendCalls throws on failure.

commit.message:
feat: add FSD (Full Speed Drive) execution mode — autopilot without safeguards

Adds "fsd" as a third ExecutionMode alongside "manual" and "autopilot".
FSD runs the autopilot loop with all safeguard checks disabled (no-progress,
resume-cap, same-action-repeat) for maximum throughput. Includes end-to-end
implementation: backend types, route validation, autopilot loop skip logic,
frontend UI toggle (3-way cycle), PauseBanner FSD labels, and comprehensive
tests for all changed components.

commit.message:
Auto-merge: claude/fix-toll-freezing-v2-aAfIp (#447)

* Fix toll freezing: add circuit breaker and show Pending instead of Unreachable

- Add circuit breaker to TollSpot API client (stops hammering dead API after 2 failures, retries after 5min cooldown)
- Change per-vehicle toll display from "Unreachable" to "Pending" with yellow/neutral styling
- Move error messaging from per-vehicle badges to a single top-level banner
- Add auto-sync on dashboard load so toll status is checked automatically
- Banner provides Retry and Clear All actions when TollSpot is down

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Trigger auto-merge workflow for toll freezing fix

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Fix circuit breaker causing alert popup on enroll

When circuit breaker is open, treat it as a network error so the vehicle
silently saves as pre_registered instead of showing a 502 error alert.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Fix stretched View Tolls and Message Host buttons in driver portal

Changed flex property from '1 1 auto' to '0 0 auto' on .booking-actions .btn
so buttons size to their content instead of growing to fill available space.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Fix vehicle tripCount never being incremented

The tripCount field was defined on the Vehicle model but never updated.
Added $inc: { tripCount: 1 } when bookings complete via:
- Return inspection completion
- Manual status change to completed
- Auto-expiration of stale active bookings

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Add tripCount backfill on server startup

Aggregates completed bookings per vehicle and sets the correct tripCount
on each vehicle. Runs once on startup after MongoDB connects.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Fix rental subtotal display on Checkout page

The rental subtotal was reverse-calculated by subtracting fees from
totalPrice, but driverProcessingFee wasn't updated after insurance
changes, causing a mismatch (e.g. $1.22 instead of $1.00).

Fix: use pricePerDay * totalDays directly (already stored on booking),
and also sync driverProcessingFee/platformFee in handleInsuranceChange.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Remove special styling from insurance row in Checkout summary

The Liability Coverage line had a distinct green/white highlight that
made it visually inconsistent with the other summary rows. Removed the
"insurance" CSS class so it matches the rest of the booking summary.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Remove manual host payout endpoints, keep weekly-only payouts

Hosts should not be able to trigger their own payouts. Removed the
transfer-earnings and transfer-all-eligible endpoints from the backend,
and replaced the "Transfer Now" button with a note that payouts are
processed automatically every Monday via the weekly scheduler.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

* Make Awaiting Payment badge clickable to resume checkout

When a booking is in awaiting_payment status, the status badge now
links to /payment/checkout?booking_id=<id> so the driver can return
to the checkout flow and complete payment.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QtQQtMDx3wMHmPXgZCrNEm

---------

feat: break SaaS conventions — poster hero, zigzag features, pipeline viz

Major layout and visual rethink to differentiate from generic SaaS:

Hero: Poster-style centered composition. Mascot IS the hero at center,
headline below, like a movie poster. Not the standard text-left/image-right.
Stats as inline pills, not a separate section.

Features: Killed the card grid entirely. Editorial zigzag strips where each
feature is a full-width alternating row (text left/right) with giant numbers,
connected by a vertical golden thread running down the center. "What's in
the Sauce" header leans into the brand metaphor.

How it Works: Horizontal pipeline visualization with connected dots and
lines instead of numbered cards. Nodes pulse on hover.

Background: Replaced blurred gradient blobs with a halftone dot pattern
(radial-gradient). More editorial, zero generic AI aesthetic.

Removed: Marquee (generic), stats box (redundant with hero stats),
feature card system (too SaaS-template).

CTA: Contained in a bordered card with halftone texture, not floating
in space with blobs behind it.

commit.message:
fix: use net reimbursement (not covered total) as expected amount in compare-eob

The compare-eob route was computing expectedReimbursement as the raw sum
of covered item amounts ($648.66) — the total BEFORE deductible and
coinsurance. GPT then set discrepancy = $648.66 - $0 = $648.66, which
got saved as eob_discrepancy and used as the appeal letter amount.

Now fetches estimated_reimbursement from pciq_analyses (the actual net
reimbursement after deductible + rate math, e.g. $18.93) and uses that
as the expected amount. Falls back to covered total if DB value missing.

Fix GDAL path setup and rock pipeline for vector GPKG input

- Prepend QGIS scripts/binaries to PATH so gdal:sieve, gdal:polygonize etc. can find their scripts
- Use gdal_calc.py directly for slope reclassification (qgis:rastercalculator broken in headless mode)

- Apply SRC_METHOD=NO_GEOTRANSFORM when loading rock raster layer
- Fix Makefile: lowercase paths for smv25 files, fix rock_%.osm target path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move openModal/showSignupForChecklist to <head> before blocking scripts

Root cause: bbCta() called openModal() which was defined at line ~3725,
AFTER two synchronous Kajabi <script src> tags that block the HTML parser.
Locally those scripts are slow/fail → openModal never defined → TypeError.

Fix: define openModal, closeModal, switchModal, showSignupForChecklist,
bbCta, bbChecklist all in the <head> script block, before any external
script can block the parser. Also remove pointer-events:none on .card *
which was unnecessary complexity.

commit.message:
fix: move renderMyScheduled inside DOMContentLoaded to restore closure access

TYPE_LABELS, TYPE_ICONS, buildTypePreview and renderMyScheduled were defined
at module scope, losing access to doConfirm/backendUrl/loadMyScheduled from
the DOMContentLoaded closure — causing the delete button to silently fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore aiman theme deleted in 4fa80465 + add SRI + remove status/evolution nav

Theme layouts, CSS assets, JS assets, and static JS files were accidentally
deleted in commit 4fa80465. This broke HTML generation — Hugo was only
building RSS/sitemap, serving stale HTML from before the deletion.

Additionally:
- Add SHA-384 SRI (Subresource Integrity) to all JS script tags via
  sri-script.html partial using Hugo's resources.FromString + fingerprint
- Remove Status and Evolution links from nav, footer, and stats widget
  (matching earlier removal from aiman-legacy theme in 3338acde)
- SRI hashes auto-compute at build time — no manual maintenance needed

Phase 3 (complete): Gateway server — AI agent, handlers, routes, boot, daemon, docs

AI module: MesoAgent with OpenAI/Anthropic enum dispatch via rig-core 0.31,
RigToolAdapter bridging MesoClaw tools to rig ToolDyn, provider factory with
API key resolution, SessionManager CRUD.

Gateway: 11 handlers (health, sessions, messages, chat, memory, config,
providers, tools, system, models, ws), 20 routes with bearer token auth
middleware, CORS, error mapping with unique MESO_* codes, WebSocket chat.

Infrastructure: boot.rs init_services() -> Services -> AppState, daemon
fully wired with graceful shutdown. 96 new tests (233 total), 0 clippy
warnings. All manual tests (M3.1-M3.5) validated.

Post-gate docs updated: architecture.md, phases.md, processes.md,
README.md, todo_tracker.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TDZ crash: playBeep/vibrate verplaatst vóór processItem

playBeep was gedeclareerd op regel 2766 maar als useCallback dependency
gebruikt op regel 1207. Dit veroorzaakt "cannot access uninitialized
variable" door de Temporal Dead Zone van const/let.

Verplaatst vibrate, soundEnabled en playBeep naar vóór processItem
zodat alle forward-references opgelost zijn.

commit.message:
Fix au regex to preserve compound words (la'au, tau'au, etc.)

Added negative lookbehind (?<!') to prevent \bau\b from matching
inside apostrophe-compound words like tau'au (shoulder), la'au (tree),
vala'au (call). Only standalone 'au' gets modernized.

Verified: 0 broken compounds, 204 collective ʻau, 1725 pronoun aʻu.

commit.message:
fix: migrate all TIMESTAMP columns to TIMESTAMPTZ for correct timezone handling

TIMESTAMP (without timezone) stored server-local time via NOW() but Rust
code treated all values as UTC, causing an offset equal to the server's
timezone. Migration converts all columns to TIMESTAMPTZ and corrects
existing data that was inconsistently stored as UTC from Rust code.

Fix checkpoint sort key crashing on Lightning -v1 version suffix

Lightning appends -v1, -v2 etc. when a checkpoint filename already exists.
The float() parse now strips the suffix before converting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix KYC AccountPE sync crash: handle dob/id_proof_expiry as string or date

The DB stores dob and id_proof_expiry as varchar(10) but the model maps
them as Date. When _sync_kyc_to_accountpe called .strftime() it crashed
because the values were strings, not date objects. This caused the KYC
sync to silently fail (falling to pending_sync) and subsequent notification
creation also failed due to the missing expires_at column.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ʻau collective noun display modernization (leading glottal stop)

Three-way au disambiguation now complete:
- ʻau (leading glottal) = collective noun: hosts/troops/group
- aʻu (medial glottal) = pronoun: I/me
- au (no glottal) = possessive: your

Added collective noun glosses for WHOLE_PHRASES (au auauna→servants,
au perofeta→prophets, au soo→disciples, au feau→messengers, etc.)
with context-aware display showing proper ʻau orthography.
